title: Moaning Lisa

jokes.text: Sorry, Dad. Women and children first.
jokes.setup: Homer is demanding bathroom access urgently
jokes.punchline: Lisa deflects with mock-chivalrous excuse from maritime disasters
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion using formal politeness to avoid bathroom pressure. Economy and misdirection work well.

jokes.text: Oh, Homer, you'd lose your head if it weren't securely fastened to your neck.
jokes.setup: Homer frantically searching for lost keys
jokes.punchline: Marge's overly literal take on the idiom about being forgetful
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom wife comment but fits Marge's patient, literal character well.

jokes.text: Warm. No, cold. Colder. Ice cold.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Bart knows where his keys are
jokes.punchline: Bart is talking about Homer's breakfast temperature, not key location
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - audience assumes it's about keys but it's breakfast. Classic Simpsons miscommunication.

jokes.text: Doh!
jokes.setup: Homer's continued frustration with missing keys
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic character catchphrase that became cultural touchstone - perfect economy and character integration

jokes.text: Well, Mom, one of us has scarfed down more than enough cupcakes over the past three decades to--
jokes.setup: Marge apologizing for only having two cupcakes for three kids
jokes.punchline: Bart starts to call out Homer's weight/eating habits before being cut off
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's blunt observation and family dynamics. Cut-off timing works well.

jokes.text: A simple cupcake will bring me no pleasure.
jokes.setup: Family cupcake distribution conflict
jokes.punchline: Lisa's melodramatic, world-weary response to dessert
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Lisa's depression storyline. Overly formal language from an 8-year-old is inherently funny.

jokes.text: La da da da da dee
jokes.setup: Class singing patriotic song in unison
jokes.punchline: Lisa improvising jazz riffs over the traditional melody
jokes.explanation: Great character establishment - Lisa's artistic rebellion against conformity. Music-based visual gag that works perfectly.

jokes.text: But Mr. Largo, that's what my country's all about.
jokes.setup: Teacher criticizing Lisa's jazz interpretation of patriotic song
jokes.punchline: Lisa earnestly defending artistic freedom as fundamentally American
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire - using patriotism to defend artistic rebellion. Perfect character voice and social commentary.

jokes.text: I'm wailing out for the homeless family living out of its car. The Iowa farmer whose land has been taken away by unfeeling bureaucrats. The West Virginia coal miner, coughing up--
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her musical interpretation
jokes.punchline: 8-year-old girl articulating complex social issues with adult sophistication
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character writing - child with adult social consciousness. Dark comedy of innocence meets harsh reality.

jokes.text: That's all fine and good, but none of those unpleasant people are going to be at the recital next week.
jokes.setup: Lisa's passionate social commentary
jokes.punchline: Teacher's callous dismissal of social problems as 'unpleasant people'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of institutional indifference. 'Unpleasant people' is perfectly dismissive and horrible.

jokes.text: And we sit around like cattle, chewing our cuds, dreading the inevitable--
jokes.setup: Lisa observing lunch period routine
jokes.punchline: Comparing school lunch to livestock feeding with existential dread
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor and philosophical observation from a child. The buildup to 'inevitable' creates perfect comedic tension.

jokes.text: Food fight!
jokes.setup: Lisa's philosophical buildup about 'dreading the inevitable'
jokes.punchline: The 'inevitable' is just a silly food fight, not existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - audience expects deep philosophical conclusion, gets juvenile chaos. Excellent comedic timing.

jokes.text: I choose not to participate.
jokes.setup: Chaotic food fight erupting around her
jokes.punchline: Lisa's formal, detached response to childish mayhem
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Lisa maintaining dignity amid chaos. Formal language creates great contrast.

jokes.text: I'm too sad.
jokes.setup: Coach demanding why she won't dodge balls
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest response that's unexpectedly profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and emotional truth. Three words that capture depression's paralyzing effect. Brilliant writing.

jokes.text: Battling Bart Simpson! And in the lavender trunks, with a record of zero wins and 48 defeats-- Oh, correction. Humiliating defeats, all of them by knockout--
jokes.setup: Bart doing boxing announcer voice for video game
jokes.punchline: Increasingly harsh description of Homer's gaming failures
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character moment. Bart's cruelty masked as entertainment. The 'correction' adds perfect extra cruelty.

jokes.text: Must you do this every time?
jokes.setup: Bart's elaborate, insulting introduction
jokes.punchline: Homer's weary acceptance of ritual humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's resigned acceptance. Implies this happens regularly.

jokes.text: Homer 'The Human Punching Bag' Simpson!
jokes.setup: Boxing announcer parody continues
jokes.punchline: Devastating nickname that's probably accurate
jokes.explanation: Cruel but accurate nickname. Works as both gaming joke and broader character commentary.

jokes.text: I didn't do it. Nobody saw me do it. There's no way they can prove anything.
jokes.setup: Note from school mentioned
jokes.punchline: Bart's instant guilty defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment. The escalating denial suggests chronic guilt and defensive preparedness.

jokes.text: It isn't? There must be some mistake.
jokes.setup: Learning the note isn't about him
jokes.punchline: Bart's confusion that school trouble could involve anyone else
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Bart assumes all school problems involve him. Shows his self-centered worldview perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, sure, I would, princess. I have feelings too, you know, like, 'My stomach hurts.' Or 'I'm going crazy!'
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Homer wouldn't understand her sadness
jokes.punchline: Homer's examples of 'feelings' are physical discomfort and mental breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - trying to be sensitive but revealing complete emotional illiteracy. The examples are hilariously basic.

jokes.text: Come on, Lisa. Ride the Homer horsey! Giddyap. Whee!
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing existential depression
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inappropriate childish response to deep sadness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Homer's desperate attempt at cheering up completely misses the mark. Shows his limited parenting toolkit.

jokes.text: Gee, Homer. Looks like you got yourself a real problem on your hands. Yeah, right. Uh, Bart, vacuum this floor.
jokes.setup: Barney pointing out Homer has a problem with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately deflecting to make Bart do chores instead of dealing with the issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer avoidance behavior and tendency to solve problems by delegation

jokes.text: Thinks he's some big stupid Homer. Oh, man. I'll show him. He thinks he's so big.
jokes.setup: Bart being forced to vacuum unfairly
jokes.punchline: Calling Homer 'some big stupid Homer' - using his name as an insult
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - 'Homer' becomes a generic insult. Shows Bart's creative vindictiveness and child logic.

jokes.text: Maggie. Come to the one you love best. / No, Maggie. Come here, girl. Come to me.
jokes.setup: Siblings competing for baby's affection
jokes.punchline: Treating baby like a pet with 'Come here, girl'
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling rivalry bit. The pet-like commands add some humor but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Come on! Don't let the-- Aaah! Oh, no, not again.
jokes.setup: Homer playing video boxing game
jokes.punchline: Homer getting knocked out repeatedly, his frustration mounting
jokes.explanation: Continuation of established running gag about Homer's gaming failures

jokes.text: Gee, Dad, you're really bad at this. I am not. It's just that I... couldn't concentrate with that infernal racket.
jokes.setup: Bart pointing out Homer's obvious failure at the game
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive excuse blaming Lisa's saxophone playing
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer deflection of blame, character-appropriate but not exceptional

jokes.text: I was just playing the blues, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer yelling at Lisa about her saxophone playing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact explanation that she's literally playing blues music
jokes.explanation: Great double meaning - she's playing blues music genre and expressing emotional blues. Perfect thematic integration.

jokes.text: Unless my fingers clacking on the keys is too loud for you.
jokes.setup: Homer asking her to continue playing
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic offer to just finger the keys silently
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intelligence and subtle sarcasm. Child's version of passive-aggression.

jokes.text: Oh, it's a little tune that I call... the 'I Never Had an Italian Suit Blues.'
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about his beautiful saxophone music
jokes.punchline: Specific, relatable blues title about modest economic circumstances
jokes.explanation: Great blues parody title. Specific and relatable economic struggle. Good character establishing moment.

jokes.text: Before you go out that door, let's put our happy face on, because people know how good a mommy you have by the size of your smile.
jokes.setup: Marge preparing Lisa for school
jokes.punchline: Marge's disturbing philosophy about performance of happiness and maternal judgment
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on social expectations and maternal pressure. Shows generational transmission of emotional suppression.

jokes.text: I am going easy on you, but you're so old and slow and weak and pathetic.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Bart to go easy on him in the game
jokes.punchline: Bart's brutal honest assessment of Homer's abilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character consistency. Bart's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly is effective.

jokes.text: I think the saddest day of my life was when I realized I could beat my dad at most things. And Bart experienced that at the age of four.
jokes.setup: Homer reflecting on getting old
jokes.punchline: Admitting his son surpassed him at age four
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about parental inadequacy and premature childhood loss. The 'age four' detail is devastating and perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, so that's it. This is some kind of underwear thing.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Lisa is becoming a young woman
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of emotional development
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - reducing complex emotional development to practical confusion. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: My friends call me Bleeding Gums. / Eww. How'd you get a name like that?
jokes.setup: Character introducing himself with unusual nickname
jokes.punchline: Child's honest grossed-out reaction
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with child's honest reaction to adult nickname. Lisa's 'Eww' is perfectly natural.

jokes.text: Well, let me put it this way. You ever been to the dentist? / Yeah. / Not me.
jokes.setup: Explaining his 'Bleeding Gums' nickname
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's never been to a dentist
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and dark humor. The contrast between child's regular dental care and adult's neglect is perfect.

jokes.text: You know, you play pretty well for someone with no real problems.
jokes.setup: Lisa finishing her blues song about her problems
jokes.punchline: Murphy's dismissal of 8-year-old problems as not 'real'
jokes.explanation: Great adult perspective on childhood problems. Shows generational gap while maintaining respect for Lisa's talent.

jokes.text: The blues isn't about feelin' better. It's about makin' other people feel worse and makin' a few bucks while you're at it.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she doesn't feel better after playing
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate description of blues music's purpose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cynical wisdom about blues music. Perfect character voice and genuinely insightful commentary on the genre.

jokes.text: Lisa! Get away from that jazzman! / But Mom! Can't I stay a little longer? / Come on. We were worried about you. Nothing personal. I just fear the unfamiliar.
jokes.setup: Marge pulling Lisa away from Murphy
jokes.punchline: Marge's honest admission about fearing unfamiliar people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character honesty about suburban prejudice. Marge's self-awareness makes it both funny and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Don't make me say it. I know the answer. You know the answer. He knows the answer. Let's just drop it. Okay?
jokes.setup: Marge pressing Bart to say he loves Lisa
jokes.punchline: Bart's elaborate avoidance of expressing emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - elaborate schemes to avoid expressing emotion. The escalation through 'everyone knows' is great.

jokes.text: Yeah. Moe's Tavern. Moe speaking. / Is Jacques there? / Who? / Jacques. Last name Strap.
jokes.setup: Bart calling bar with fake name request
jokes.punchline: Classic prank call setup with 'Jock Strap' name
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call formula. Not original but executed perfectly and became iconic for the series.

jokes.text: Aw, wait a minute. Jacques Strap? It's you, isn't it, you cowardly little runt! When I get a hold of you, I am gonna gut you like a fish and drink your blood.
jokes.setup: Moe realizing he's been pranked
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent threats over a phone prank
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and character establishment for Moe. Ridiculously over-the-top threats for minor prank.

jokes.text: Howie! I thought I told you to stop wasting your money in this stupid place! / Sorry, Mom. / And you! A man of your age. You should be ashamed of yourself.
jokes.setup: Homer getting boxing tips from a kid
jokes.punchline: Being shamed by the kid's mother for immature behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Homer's immaturity. Adult being scolded alongside child is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Take all your bad feelings and push them down. All the way down, past your knees, until you're almost walking on them.
jokes.setup: Marge teaching Lisa to smile despite sadness
jokes.punchline: Disturbingly specific metaphor for emotional suppression
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark metaphor for emotional suppression. The physical imagery of 'walking on' feelings is disturbing and perfect.

jokes.text: I feel more popular already.
jokes.setup: Lisa forced to smile despite depression
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response to immediate 'improvement'
jokes.explanation: Good character response showing Lisa's intelligence and resistance to false solutions. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: You know, I used to think you were some brainiac, but I guess you're okay.
jokes.setup: Lisa forcing herself to act 'normal'
jokes.punchline: Classmate's backhanded compliment revealing how intelligence is viewed negatively
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary on how intelligence is stigmatized, especially in young girls

jokes.text: Lisa, I apologize to you. I was wrong. I take it all back. Always be yourself. You wanna be sad, honey, be sad.
jokes.setup: Marge seeing Lisa's forced performance
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of previous advice after seeing results
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment. Marge learning from mistake and correcting course. Emotional honesty.

jokes.text: Marge, how could you? I was so close. / I'm sorry, but this is more important than that silly, loud game.
jokes.setup: Marge interrupting Homer's video game victory
jokes.punchline: Clash between Homer's gaming obsession and family importance
jokes.explanation: Good character priorities conflict. Shows Homer's skewed values but also Marge's wisdom.

jokes.text: My game, my game! I could have beat the boy.
jokes.setup: Homer finally getting the upper hand in video boxing
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic desperation over a meaningless video game victory
jokes.explanation: Pathetic but relatable moment showing Homer's desperate need for any victory over his son

jokes.text: You're right, Mom. I'd just like to use this occasion to announce my retirement, undefeated, from the world of video boxing.
jokes.setup: Bart's gaming dominance being interrupted
jokes.punchline: Declaring retirement to preserve undefeated record
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bart's strategic thinking and ego protection. Sports retirement parody works perfectly.

tmdb_overview: The meaning of life and second grade have Lisa playing the blues solo, until she jams with a kindred spirit; Homer seeks tutelage to beat Bart at video boxing.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Janey Powell / Boy with Howie / Howie's Mother (voice)

insight: Early Simpsons balances sadness with 49 jokes—character work outweighs punchline precision here.
